---
title: "Playful Teaching of Simulation Models: From Monolithic Shiny Apps to Quarto Dashboards and webR"
author: "Thomas Petzoldt and Johannes Feldbauer"
date: "July 09 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, styles.scss]
    footer: "Petzoldt & Feldbauer | useR!2026 | {{< meta date >}}"
    css:
      - footer-logo-left.css
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-visibility: "hidden"
---

## {.title-page}

::::: title-slide-header
![](tu_dresden_logo.svg){.logo-left}

![](hydrobiology_logo.svg){.logo-right}
:::::

::::: title-slide-body
:::: title-slide-left

[Playful Teaching<br> of Simulation Models:<br> From Monolithic Shiny Apps to Quarto Dashboards and webR]{.big-title}

[Thomas Petzoldt and<br> Johannes Feldbauer]{.blue}

::::

:::: title-slide-right
![](climate-explorer.png){.title-illustration-img}
::::
:::::

# The Original Challenge

## Complex Systems 

Content here...

* lorem ipsum
* foo foo bar bar bar



## Qualified People?

![Search for qualified students who are interested in ecology **and** modeling](img/unitag.jpg)

## Current Situation

<br>

$\Rightarrow$ Modeling and data science became an important part of limnology.

<br>


**Demand in Research and Engineering**

* people who can [use]{.cyan} computers
* people who can [understand]{.cyan} models
* people who are able to [develop]{.cyan} models

**School Experience** 

* [mathematics]{.neongreen} is complicated
* [programming]{.neongreen} is something for nerds 
* differential [calculus]{.neongreen} is rocket science

**Domain knowledge** 

<!-- channge this if for another conference -->
* limnology, of course 😉


## Web-based Apps with Shiny

<br>

* Interactivity: Playful exploration of dynamic systems
* Data: Direct access to test cases and real world data
* Algorithms: full **R** ecosystemavailable

→ No installation hurdles, no Excel debugging, no problems with csv2

<br>

## Use for Teaching and Project Communication

<br>

1. General Public
2. Interested public
3. People with domain specific education
4. Specialists

<br>

**For example ...**

* School kids and their parents
* People who want to see the facts of climate warming
* Participants of an ecological conference
* Reservoir management professionals



## Example 1: Lake Profile Plotter

<br></br>

::: {.column width="49%"}
* Analysis of multiparameter probe data
    - Complex interactions between temperature, light, algae, oxygen, pH, conductivity
    - Excel data structures, plotting, trend line, colors, ... distract from content
* Concentrate on scientific background and data interpretation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![[https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/lakeplot/](https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/lakeplot/)](img/lakeplot.png)
:::

$\Rightarrow$ Has also passed its acid test in field courses.

## Example 2: Climate Data Explorer

<!---
The image is rather small, but the idea is to either:

- zoom in with Alt + Mouse click to center of Zoom region
- open link: it opens in a new browser tab, then move back by selecting the presentation tab
--->

<br></br>

::: {.column width="33%"}

<br></br>

* Visualisation of climate data and trends
* Direct access to data from the [German Weather Service](https://www.dwd.de)
* Used in teaching and for public outreach
:::

::: {.column width="66%"}
![[https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/dwd-trends/](https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/dwd-trends/)](img/dwd-trends.png)
:::

## Example 3: Reservoir Management with a hydrophysical model

<br></br>

::: {.column width="33%"}

<br></br>

* 1D hydrophysical model GOTM provided as web service
* Predefined scenarios
* Quick performance
* Results can be downloaded
:::

::: {.column width="66%"}
![[https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/talsperre/](https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/talsperre/)](img/talsperre.png)
:::


# Is everything solved now?

# No

## "Classical" Shiny Apps

<br>

**Technical Fragmentation: Zoo of Apps**

* deployment: server load / load balancing, security, OS updates
* apps need also regular updates and maintenance
* each app has its own philosophy, package dependencies etc.


**Didactical:  Media Fragmentation**

* Small self-explanatory apps -- limited complexity
* Complex stand-alone apps: 
  - difficult to integrate explanation and code, 
  - creating clean structure -- high effort for the developer 
  - narratives within the code -- overwhelming for the user
  - need explanation by instructor and/or handouts on paper
* Storyboards -- mostly linear, more "show" than learning effect


# Approach

## Step 1: Integrated Dashboard with Quarto

* Unified UI: Consistent layout and interaction
  - intentionally simple
  - reduce confusion of the user
* Integrated: narrative + interactive code + vizusalization
  - Explanatory text, tasks and outlook at different levels
  - Supports active, scaffolded learning and immediate feedback
  - Self-explaining, exploratory learning: Learners can engage deeply without relying on external handouts or instructor intervention.
* Modular: maintainable code and narrative text: 
  - Each tab implemented in a separate file
  - Easier updates and scalability
  - Modularity allows easier internationalization using AI tools
  - Top-hierarchy as Quarto document
  
  
## Demo

[https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/simbiose-w/de/](https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/app/simbiose-w/de/)
